steps.title: Find the semicircle radius
steps.content:
Since $K$ is the midpoint of segment $JL$ and $KL=13$, $JL=26$. Point $L$ is the center, so $LM$ is a radius. Because $JM$ is the diameter,
$$2r=26+r$$
$$r=26$$

steps.title: Find the rectangular area
steps.content:
The rectangle's base is
$$KM=KL+LM=13+26=39$$
Its height is $14$, so its area is
$$39\cdot14=546$$

steps.title: Add the semicircle area
steps.content:
The semicircle area is
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2=338\pi$$
The total area is
$$546+338\pi$$
The answer is C.
